Title: Impacts of environmental conditions, and allelic variation of cytosolic glutamine synthetase on maize hybrid kernel production

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase family; cytosolic GS1 isoenzyme
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: A188-derived transgene construct / maize native gene
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hybrids derived from A188 × RBO1; also AAX3 and AAX7 tester crosses; transgenic events H12, H14, H17, H18, H20, H22, H23, H27, H31, H32, H39, H40
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: Cassava vein mosaic virus promoter (CsVMV, constitutive/mesophyll-active) fused to rice actin1 first intron; maize Rubisco small subunit promoter RbcS (bundle sheath-active)
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native GS1-3 isoenzyme present in leaf mesophyll cells
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Overexpressed in leaf mesophyll cells and bundle sheath cells; also detected in phloem companion cells; assayed at vegetative stage and 15 days after silking
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary ammonium assimilation and reassimilation in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a key control step/potential lead for kernel yield and nitrogen use efficiency, but not explicitly established as sole rate-limiting step
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent incorporation of ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for amino acid/nitrogen assimilation that supports kernel production and kernel nitrogen filling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Amino acid / agronomic yield trait linked to nitrogen metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Organic nitrogen transport and assimilation; supports kernel set and grain filling
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Accumulation_Site: Leaves for glutamine synthesis; kernels for yield and increased total N content
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field environments across 5 years and 10 USA locations; optimal N fertilization; moderate water deficit in some sites; 15NH4+ feeding in detached leaves
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Transgenic overexpression in field-grown hybrids; leaf GS activity assay; protein gel blot; immunolocalization; 15NH4+ labeling and amino acid profiling; multi-location field yield trials
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Overexpression increased leaf GS1 activity about 2-fold at vegetative stage and about 3-fold at 15 DAS, increased glutamine synthesis, increased kernel N content, and increased kernel yield on average by 3.8% across environments
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted association genetics using MAGIC panel SNP data; genotypic data available at EVA ERZ3038796 and project PRJEB46968
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gln1-3 overexpression in maize leaves increased cytosolic GS1 protein/activity in mesophyll and bundle sheath cells. 2. Enhanced GS1 increased 15NH4+ incorporation and doubled glutamine accumulation in tested transgenic hybrids. 3. Across 14 field experiments, Gln1-3 overexpression increased kernel yield on average by 3.8%, with stronger effects in some environments. 4. Promoter-region polymorphisms in Gln1-3 were significantly associated with kernel yield in several environments.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Promising target for transgenic enhancement or marker-assisted selection to improve maize hybrid yield and NUE; deployment should consider environment-specific performance and event selection
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Yield benefit was environment-dependent and event-dependent; increased GS activity did not always correlate with yield gain; no major differences in vegetative dry matter; no reported grain moisture penalty
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: Gln1-3 protein is 98% identical to Gln1-4 and was used to drive overexpression in both mesophyll and bundle sheath cells using dual promoters.

Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Glutamine synthetase family; cytosolic GS1 isoenzyme
Pathway_Genes.Source_Species_Variety: B73 reference used for SNP positions; native maize allele variation analyzed in MAGIC founders
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: MAGIC-derived DHIL testcross hybrids with tester MBS847; also discussed in maize hybrids generally
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Native GS1-4 isoenzyme specifically localized in bundle sheath cells
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: Primary ammonium assimilation and reassimilation in leaves
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: Supported as a candidate key control enzyme influencing kernel yield via allelic variation
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Catalyzes ATP-dependent incorporation of ammonium into glutamate to form glutamine
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: Supplies precursor pool for nitrogen assimilation associated with kernel yield
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Class: Agronomic yield trait linked to amino acid/nitrogen metabolism
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Supports nitrogen assimilation related to kernel productivity
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Association mapping across environments in Blois, Saint-Paul, and Nerac during 2015-2017
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Association genetics in MAGIC panel; SNP-trait association with kernel yield at 15% moisture
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Nine linked polymorphisms in 3'UTR/exon10/intron4 of Gln1-4 were significantly associated with increased kernel yield in specific environments; favorable alleles associated with about 6.5% increase in KY
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: Targeted association genetics using MAGIC panel SNP data; EVA ERZ3038796; PRJEB46968
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Gln1-4 encodes a bundle sheath-localized cytosolic GS1 isoenzyme. 2. Nine TAS markers in Gln1-4 were significantly associated with kernel yield in specific test environments. 3. Favorable alleles were associated with an approximately 6.5% increase in kernel yield, supporting breeding utility.
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful marker-assisted selection target for improving maize hybrid yield; environmental dependency of association should be considered
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Association signals were environment-specific and not detected in all trials
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: No transgenic manipulation of Gln1-4 was performed in this paper; evidence is from allelic association only.
